Body:
  # Cartograph: collapse companion docs into `writ.ext['cartograph']`
  
  ## Intent
  
  The Cartograph apparatus today maintains three companion books — `cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, `cartograph/pieces` — each holding a `{id, stage, codex, createdAt, updatedAt}` document keyed one-for-one to a writ id. This pattern predates the writ-level `ext` slot. Now that `writ.ext` exists as a sanctioned plugin-keyed metadata slot with a transactional read-modify-write API, those companion books carry pure metadata that can live directly on the writ row.
  
  This commission collapses the three companion books into a single `writ.ext['cartograph'] = { stage, codex }` sub-slot. The cartograph's typed-API surface stays — every existing callable continues to work with the same arguments and return shapes — but the internals shed three Stacks books, three companion-doc shapes, and the dual-write transactional ceremony around them. `createdAt` / `updatedAt` were always duplicates of the writ's own timestamps; they go away.
  
  ## Motivation
  
  - **The companion-doc pattern is the pre-`ext` workaround for plugin-owned writ metadata.** Now that `ext` exists as the sanctioned slot, a pure-metadata companion book is exactly what `ext` was added to replace.
  - **The substrate work that depends on the cartograph (the surveyor-apparatus, see Commission C in the surveying-cascade design subtree) is best designed against the post-cleanup shape.** Building the substrate's CDC observer, completion-stamping logic, and rig routing on top of the current companion-doc pattern, then reshaping it later, is strictly more work than landing the cleanup first. The substrate's CDC observer becomes a writ-type-filtered subscription (a primitive Stacks already supports cleanly) rather than a per-companion-book subscription.
  - **No external behavioral change is intended.** Every consumer (CLI tools, tests, the apply CLI shipped in Commission A) reads the same shape from the same API. The cleanup is internal.
  
  ## Non-negotiable decisions
  
  ### 1. `ext['cartograph']` is the canonical home for stage and codex
  
  ```typescript
  interface CartographExt {
    stage: VisionStage | ChargeStage | PieceStage;  // type depends on the writ type
    codex?: string;
  }
  ```
  
  The plugin id is `cartograph`. The slot is plugin-keyed under the established convention; the cartograph apparatus is the only writer.
  
  ### 2. The typed-API surface stays stable
  
  `CartographApi` keeps every method it has today:
  
  - `createVision` / `createCharge` / `createPiece`
  - `showVision` / `showCharge` / `showPiece`
  - `listVisions` / `listCharges` / `listPieces`
  - `patchVision` / `patchCharge` / `patchPiece`
  - `transitionVision` / `transitionCharge` / `transitionPiece`
  
  Argument shapes (`CreateVisionRequest`, `TransitionChargeRequest`, etc.) and return shapes (`VisionDoc`, `ChargeDoc`, `PieceDoc`) stay. The doc shapes are projections over `writ.ext['cartograph']` rather than rows in a separate book — but from the caller's view, nothing changes.
  
  `createdAt` and `updatedAt` on the projection map to the writ's own timestamps.
  
  ### 3. Atomicity for create + transition
  
  `clerk.transition` silently strips `ext` from its body (per the existing Clerk contract). Any operation that touches both `writ.phase` and `writ.ext['cartograph']` MUST wrap the `clerk.transition` and `clerk.setWritExt` calls in a single `stacks.transaction` so the pair is atomic. This applies to `createX` (post + setWritExt) and `transitionX` (transition + setWritExt) alike.
  
  The existing `createVision({ stage })` parameter — which Commission A relied on for the single-event-per-apply guarantee — keeps its semantic: the cartograph writes the writ at its initial phase, stamps `ext['cartograph']` with the supplied stage, and (if the stage isn't the writ-type's initial state) transitions to it, all inside one `stacks.transaction`.
  
  ### 4. Read-side projection
  
  `showVision(id)` / `listVisions(filters)` continue to return `VisionDoc`-shaped values. Internally they:
  
  - read the writ via `clerk.show(id)` (or `clerk.list({ type: 'vision' })`)
  - project `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage` and `writ.ext['cartograph'].codex` onto the returned shape
  - carry the writ's `createdAt` and `updatedAt` directly
  
  The existing `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` exported types stay as projection shapes (their definitions can be retained verbatim — the only difference is where the data comes from). Filtering on `stage` becomes a JSON-field filter against `writ.ext['cartograph'].stage`; Stacks' query layer supports this.
  
  ### 5. Companion-book deletion
  
  `cartograph/visions`, `cartograph/charges`, and `cartograph/pieces` Stacks books are removed entirely. There is no migration of historical companion-doc rows — the cartograph is recently-shipped and nothing in production-style guild data depends on those books. Any test fixtures or integration tests that opened the companion books directly should be updated to read via the typed API.
  
  ### 6. Surveying-cascade arch doc updates
  
  The settled architecture for the surveying cascade (`docs/architecture/surveying-cascade.md`) names the companion-doc pattern explicitly in three sections. Update them to reflect the post-cleanup shape:
  
  - **§3.4 — Companion `SurveyDoc` holds envelope metadata only.** SurveyDoc was modelled on the cartograph companion-doc pattern. Rewrite this section so the survey-writ envelope metadata (rigVersion, surveyorId, completedAt — minus targetNodeId, which is already `writ.parentId`, and minus rigName, which is already `writ.type`) lives in `status['surveyor']` / `ext['surveyor']` sub-slots on the survey writ rather than in a `books.surveys` companion book. Note that `completedAt` is redundant with `writ.resolvedAt`. The substrate is the only writer.
  
  - **§3.6 — Substrate watches cartograph CDC.** The current text lists three book-event streams (`book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.charges.{created,updated}`, `book.cartograph.pieces.{created,updated}`). Update to a single subscription on the writs book filtered by `writ.type ∈ {vision, charge, piece}`. The single-event-per-apply guarantee discussion stays — it shifts from "wrap createVision + transition in one transaction" to "the cartograph's createX/transitionX primitives are already transactional" with the same outcome (one CDC-significant phase transition per apply).
  
  - **§3.7 — Substrate plugin shape.** The substrate-owned-things list includes "Owns survey writ types and `books.surveys`." Drop `books.surveys` from the list. The substrate now owns the `status['surveyor']` and `ext['surveyor']` slots on survey writs instead. Likewise drop "Stamps SurveyDoc on completion" and replace with "Stamps `status['surveyor']` on completion."
  
  These three sections are the only load-bearing references; minor incidental references elsewhere in the doc may need to be touched up for consistency.
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - **Migrating SurveyDoc itself.** The surveyor-apparatus doesn't exist yet (Commission C in the surveying-cascade design subtree). This commission updates the arch doc to reflect the post-cleanup shape so Commission C is briefed against it, but does not build the substrate.
  - **Migrating other companion-doc patterns elsewhere in the framework.** The Astrolabe `PlanDoc` and similar are out of scope; PlanDoc is mostly content (inventory, scope, decisions, spec) and isn't a metadata-only companion.
  - **Reworking the `ext` API itself.** `clerk.setWritExt` and the slot-write contract are existing primitives; this commission consumes them, doesn't extend them.
  - **Renaming `VisionDoc` / `ChargeDoc` / `PieceDoc` projection types.** They keep their names and shapes — only their backing storage moves.
  
  ## References
  
  - Parent design subtree: `c-moji050w` — Cartograph + surveying cascade.
  - Downstream consumer: `c-moji0ggh` — Commission C, the surveyor-apparatus substrate, which depends on this cleanup landing first.
  - The `setWritExt` API contract is documented on `ClerkApi` in `clerk-apparatus`'s public types — that's the load-bearing primitive.

1	/**
2	 * Cartograph — public types.
3	 *
4	 * The Cartograph contributes three writ types to the Clerk —
5	 * `vision`, `charge`, and `piece` — and shadows each one with a
6	 * typed companion document stored in its own book.
7	 *
8	 * Companion-doc convention follows astrolabe's `PlanDoc`: the doc id
9	 * matches the writ id one-for-one; both rows are written under one
10	 * `stacks.transaction(...)` boundary by `createX`. Vision text lives on
11	 * `writ.body`; the companion doc carries typed metadata only — the
12	 * minimal field set is deliberate (D14 in the commission spec).
13	 *
14	 * Stage enums are per-type so each carries the domain-level vocabulary
15	 * relevant to its level of the decomposition ladder (see D10 in the
16	 * commission spec).
17	 */
18	
19	import type { WritPhase } from '@shardworks/clerk-apparatus';
20	
21	// ── Stage enums (per type) ───────────────────────────────────────────
22	
23	/**
24	 * Lifecycle stage on a `VisionDoc`. Captures the patron-facing meaning
25	 * of a vision's progression separately from the writ's `phase`:
26	 *
27	 *  - `draft`     — the vision is being shaped; not yet active.
28	 *  - `active`    — the vision is in flight.
29	 *  - `sunset`    — the patron retired the vision (graceful wind-down).
30	 *  - `cancelled` — the vision was cancelled before reaching `sunset`.
31	 *
32	 * The typed-API `transitionVision` helper is the only sanctioned path
33	 * that updates this field; it writes both `writ.phase` and `stage`
34	 * inside one transaction so the two never drift.
35	 */
36	export type VisionStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'sunset' | 'cancelled';
37	
38	/**
39	 * Lifecycle stage on a `ChargeDoc`. The `validated` value records the
40	 * patron-walkthrough acceptance moment that distinguishes a charge from
41	 * a piece:
42	 *
43	 *  - `draft`      — the charge is being shaped.
44	 *  - `active`     — the charge is in flight under its parent vision.
45	 *  - `validated`  — the patron walked through and accepted the charge.
46	 *  - `dropped`    — the charge was abandoned without acceptance.
47	 */
48	export type ChargeStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'validated' | 'dropped';
49	
50	/**
51	 * Lifecycle stage on a `PieceDoc`. Pieces are internal organization;
52	 * the `done` value records completion as a structural fact rather than
53	 * a patron-walkthrough fact:
54	 *
55	 *  - `draft`   — the piece is being shaped.
56	 *  - `active`  — the piece is in flight.
57	 *  - `done`    — the piece is complete.
58	 *  - `dropped` — the piece was abandoned.
59	 */
60	export type PieceStage = 'draft' | 'active' | 'done' | 'dropped';
61	
62	// ── Companion documents ──────────────────────────────────────────────
63	
64	/**
65	 * Companion document for a `vision` writ. Keyed by the writ id one-for-
66	 * one. Carries typed metadata only — vision text lives on `writ.body`.
67	 *
68	 * The `[key: string]: unknown` index signature satisfies Stacks'
69	 * `BookEntry` constraint and lets future commissions grow the field set
70	 * non-breakingly. The patch surface mirrors astrolabe's `PlanDoc.patch`.
71	 */
72	export interface VisionDoc {
73	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry. */
74	  [key: string]: unknown;
75	  /** The vision writ's id — primary key, matches the writ id. */
76	  id: string;
77	  /** Lifecycle stage. Coupled to `writ.phase` by the typed-API transition helpers. */
78	  stage: VisionStage;
79	  /** Codex this vision targets. Inherited from the writ at creation time. */
80	  codex?: string;
81	  /** ISO timestamp when the doc was created. */
82	  createdAt: string;
83	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
84	  updatedAt: string;
85	}
86	
87	/**
88	 * Companion document for a `charge` writ. Same shape rules as
89	 * `VisionDoc` — keyed by writ id, minimal fields, index signature for
90	 * forward compatibility.
91	 */
92	export interface ChargeDoc {
93	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry. */
94	  [key: string]: unknown;
95	  /** The charge writ's id — primary key, matches the writ id. */
96	  id: string;
97	  /** Lifecycle stage. Coupled to `writ.phase` by the typed-API transition helpers. */
98	  stage: ChargeStage;
99	  /** Codex this charge targets. Inherited from the writ at creation time. */
100	  codex?: string;
101	  /** ISO timestamp when the doc was created. */
102	  createdAt: string;
103	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
104	  updatedAt: string;
105	}
106	
107	/**
108	 * Companion document for a `piece` writ. Same shape rules as
109	 * `VisionDoc`/`ChargeDoc` — keyed by writ id, minimal fields, index
110	 * signature for forward compatibility.
111	 */
112	export interface PieceDoc {
113	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry. */
114	  [key: string]: unknown;
115	  /** The piece writ's id — primary key, matches the writ id. */
116	  id: string;
117	  /** Lifecycle stage. Coupled to `writ.phase` by the typed-API transition helpers. */
118	  stage: PieceStage;
119	  /** Codex this piece targets. Inherited from the writ at creation time. */
120	  codex?: string;
121	  /** ISO timestamp when the doc was created. */
122	  createdAt: string;
123	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
124	  updatedAt: string;
125	}
126	
127	// ── Filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
128	
129	/**
130	 * Filters for listing visions. Mirrors astrolabe's `PlanFilters` shape
131	 * with `stage` replacing `status`. Lists are ordered by `createdAt desc`.
132	 */
133	export interface VisionFilters {
134	  /** Filter by stage. */
135	  stage?: VisionStage;
136	  /** Filter by codex. */
137	  codex?: string;
138	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
139	  limit?: number;
140	  /** Number of results to skip. */
141	  offset?: number;
142	}
143	
144	/** Filters for listing charges. Mirrors `VisionFilters`. */
145	export interface ChargeFilters {
146	  /** Filter by stage. */
147	  stage?: ChargeStage;
148	  /** Filter by codex. */
149	  codex?: string;
150	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
151	  limit?: number;
152	  /** Number of results to skip. */
153	  offset?: number;
154	}
155	
156	/** Filters for listing pieces. Mirrors `VisionFilters`. */
157	export interface PieceFilters {
158	  /** Filter by stage. */
159	  stage?: PieceStage;
160	  /** Filter by codex. */
161	  codex?: string;
162	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
163	  limit?: number;
164	  /** Number of results to skip. */
165	  offset?: number;
166	}
167	
168	// ── Create requests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
169	
170	/**
171	 * Request to create a top-level vision. Visions have no parent — passing
172	 * a non-empty `parentId` is rejected by the typed API.
173	 *
174	 * The optional `phase` and `stage` fields let a caller (e.g.
175	 * `nsg vision apply`) bootstrap a vision directly into an active state
176	 * inside the same atomic transaction that creates the writ row and the
177	 * companion VisionDoc. The phase/stage pair must match the fixed mapping
178	 * the typed API maintains for initial creation:
179	 *
180	 *   - phase `'new'`  paired with stage `'draft'`  (default)
181	 *   - phase `'open'` paired with stage `'active'`
182	 *
183	 * Terminal initial states (`sunset` / `cancelled`) are rejected — a vision
184	 * cannot be born retired. To retire a vision, create it then call
185	 * `transitionVision`.
186	 */
187	export interface CreateVisionRequest {
188	  /** Short human-readable title describing the vision. */
189	  title: string;
190	  /** Long-form vision text, stored on `writ.body`. */
191	  body: string;
192	  /** Optional target codex. */
193	  codex?: string;
194	  /**
195	   * Optional initial phase on the underlying writ. Defaults to `'new'`.
196	   * Must be paired with a matching `stage` per the initial-state mapping.
197	   */
198	  phase?: WritPhase;
199	  /**
200	   * Optional initial stage on the companion VisionDoc. Defaults to
201	   * `'draft'`. Must pair with `phase` per the initial-state mapping.
202	   * Terminal stages (`sunset`, `cancelled`) are rejected.
203	   */
204	  stage?: VisionStage;
205	}
206	
207	/**
208	 * Request to create a charge under a vision. The typed API rejects when
209	 * `parentId` is missing or when the parent's writ type is not `vision`.
210	 */
211	export interface CreateChargeRequest {
212	  /** Required: must reference an existing vision in a non-terminal state. */
213	  parentId: string;
214	  /** Short human-readable title describing the charge. */
215	  title: string;
216	  /** Detail text, stored on `writ.body`. */
217	  body: string;
218	  /** Optional target codex. Defaults to the parent vision's codex. */
219	  codex?: string;
220	}
221	
222	/**
223	 * Request to create a piece under a charge or piece. The typed API
224	 * rejects when `parentId` is missing or when the parent's writ type is
225	 * not `charge` or `piece`.
226	 */
227	export interface CreatePieceRequest {
228	  /** Required: must reference an existing charge or piece in a non-terminal state. */
229	  parentId: string;
230	  /** Short human-readable title describing the piece. */
231	  title: string;
232	  /** Detail text, stored on `writ.body`. */
233	  body: string;
234	  /** Optional target codex. Defaults to the parent's codex. */
235	  codex?: string;
236	}
237	
238	// ── Transition requests ──────────────────────────────────────────────
239	
240	/**
241	 * Lifecycle-coupled transition request for a vision. The caller supplies
242	 * both the target `phase` and the target `stage` because the mapping is
243	 * not always one-to-one — for example, a vision driven to `failed` might
244	 * mean stage `cancelled` or stage `sunset` depending on context. The
245	 * typed API writes both fields atomically.
246	 */
247	export interface TransitionVisionRequest {
248	  /** Target phase on the underlying writ. */
249	  phase: WritPhase;
250	  /** Target stage on the companion VisionDoc. */
251	  stage: VisionStage;
252	  /** Optional resolution string. Set on terminal transitions. */
253	  resolution?: string;
254	}
255	
256	/**
257	 * Lifecycle-coupled transition request for a charge. See
258	 * `TransitionVisionRequest` for why both `phase` and `stage` are
259	 * specified explicitly.
260	 */
261	export interface TransitionChargeRequest {
262	  /** Target phase on the underlying writ. */
263	  phase: WritPhase;
264	  /** Target stage on the companion ChargeDoc. */
265	  stage: ChargeStage;
266	  /** Optional resolution string. Set on terminal transitions. */
267	  resolution?: string;
268	}
269	
270	/**
271	 * Lifecycle-coupled transition request for a piece. See
272	 * `TransitionVisionRequest` for why both `phase` and `stage` are
273	 * specified explicitly.
274	 */
275	export interface TransitionPieceRequest {
276	  /** Target phase on the underlying writ. */
277	  phase: WritPhase;
278	  /** Target stage on the companion PieceDoc. */
279	  stage: PieceStage;
280	  /** Optional resolution string. Set on terminal transitions. */
281	  resolution?: string;
282	}
283	
284	// ── API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
285	
286	/**
287	 * The `provides` interface for the Cartograph apparatus.
288	 *
289	 * The typed-API surface is the **only** layer that enforces the ladder's
290	 * parent invariants:
291	 *
292	 *   - `createVision` rejects any non-empty `parentId`.
293	 *   - `createCharge` rejects unless the parent's writ type is `vision`.
294	 *   - `createPiece`  rejects unless the parent's writ type is `charge` or `piece`.
295	 *
296	 * Raw `clerk.post({ type: 'vision' })` continues to succeed without
297	 * parent-type checks — the typed API is the validator. WritTypeConfig
298	 * deliberately carries no parentTypes/allowedChildren restrictions.
299	 *
300	 * Each `createX` opens a single Stacks transaction and writes both the
301	 * writ row and the companion document inside one boundary. Parent
302	 * existence, parent-not-terminal, codex inheritance, and id generation
303	 * mirror Clerk's `post()` validation byte-for-byte (the duplication is
304	 * the cost of being a typed atomic surface).
305	 *
306	 * Each `transitionX` writes both `writ.phase` and the companion doc's
307	 * `stage` field atomically. The caller specifies both targets
308	 * explicitly because a single phase may map to multiple stages
309	 * depending on context.
310	 */
311	export interface CartographApi {
312	  // ── Vision ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
313	
314	  /** Create a top-level vision. Rejects when `parentId` is set. */
315	  createVision(request: CreateVisionRequest): Promise<VisionDoc>;
316	  /** Show a vision by writ id. Throws if the doc is not found. */
317	  showVision(id: string): Promise<VisionDoc>;
318	  /** List visions, ordered by createdAt descending. */
319	  listVisions(filters?: VisionFilters): Promise<VisionDoc[]>;
320	  /** Patch a vision's companion doc. Returns the updated document. */
321	  patchVision(id: string, fields: Partial<Omit<VisionDoc, 'id'>>): Promise<VisionDoc>;
322	  /** Atomically transition both the writ phase and the companion stage. */
323	  transitionVision(id: string, request: TransitionVisionRequest): Promise<VisionDoc>;
324	
325	  // ── Charge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
326	
327	  /** Create a charge under a vision. Rejects when the parent is not a vision. */
328	  createCharge(request: CreateChargeRequest): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
329	  /** Show a charge by writ id. Throws if the doc is not found. */
330	  showCharge(id: string): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
331	  /** List charges, ordered by createdAt descending. */
332	  listCharges(filters?: ChargeFilters): Promise<ChargeDoc[]>;
333	  /** Patch a charge's companion doc. Returns the updated document. */
334	  patchCharge(id: string, fields: Partial<Omit<ChargeDoc, 'id'>>): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
335	  /** Atomically transition both the writ phase and the companion stage. */
336	  transitionCharge(id: string, request: TransitionChargeRequest): Promise<ChargeDoc>;
337	
338	  // ── Piece ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
339	
340	  /** Create a piece under a charge or piece. Rejects on any other parent type. */
341	  createPiece(request: CreatePieceRequest): Promise<PieceDoc>;
342	  /** Show a piece by writ id. Throws if the doc is not found. */
343	  showPiece(id: string): Promise<PieceDoc>;
344	  /** List pieces, ordered by createdAt descending. */
345	  listPieces(filters?: PieceFilters): Promise<PieceDoc[]>;
346	  /** Patch a piece's companion doc. Returns the updated document. */
347	  patchPiece(id: string, fields: Partial<Omit<PieceDoc, 'id'>>): Promise<PieceDoc>;
348	  /** Atomically transition both the writ phase and the companion stage. */
349	  transitionPiece(id: string, request: TransitionPieceRequest): Promise<PieceDoc>;
350	}
351

1	/**
2	 * @shardworks/cartograph-apparatus — The Cartograph.
3	 *
4	 * Decomposition-ladder data substrate: contributes the `vision`, `charge`,

6	 * enforces the ladder's parent invariants.
7	 *
8	 * See: README.md
9	 */

12

1	import { z } from 'zod';
2	import { guild } from '@shardworks/nexus-core';
3	import { tool } from '@shardworks/tools-apparatus';
4	import type { CartographApi } from '../types.ts';
5	import type { ClerkApi } from '@shardworks/clerk-apparatus';
6	
7	/**
8	 * Allowed writ phases across all writ types (mirrors `WritPhase` from
9	 * the Clerk API). Required at the Zod boundary so `--help` enumerates
10	 * the legal values (D16). The typed API still enforces per-state
11	 * `allowedTransitions` and rejects illegal edges.
12	 */
13	const WRIT_PHASES = ['new', 'open', 'stuck', 'completed', 'failed', 'cancelled'] as const;
14	
15	/**
16	 * Vision-specific stage enum (mirrors `VisionStage` from `../types.ts`).
17	 * Per D15 the caller supplies both phase and stage explicitly because a
18	 * single phase may map to multiple stages depending on context.
19	 */
20	const VISION_STAGES = ['draft', 'active', 'sunset', 'cancelled'] as const;
21	
22	/**
23	 * Transition a vision: writes both `writ.phase` and the companion doc's
24	 * `stage` atomically inside one Stacks transaction. Both `--phase` and
25	 * `--stage` are required (D15) and Zod-enum-validated (D16). Optional
26	 * `--resolution` lands in the same transaction (D17).
27	 */
28	export default tool({
29	  name: 'vision-transition',
30	  description: 'Transition a vision lifecycle (writes phase + stage atomically)',
31	  instructions:
32	    'Atomically updates the vision writ\'s phase and the companion doc\'s stage. ' +
33	    'Both --phase and --stage are required: a single phase may map to multiple ' +
34	    'stages depending on context (e.g. failed → cancelled vs failed → sunset). ' +
35	    'The typed API rejects illegal phase edges per the writ-type config.',
36	  params: {
37	    id: z.string().describe('Vision id (or short prefix)'),
38	    phase: z.enum(WRIT_PHASES).describe('Target phase on the underlying writ'),
39	    stage: z.enum(VISION_STAGES).describe('Target stage on the companion vision doc'),
40	    resolution: z
41	      .string()
42	      .optional()
43	      .describe('Optional resolution string. Recorded on terminal transitions.'),
44	  },
45	  permission: 'write',
46	  callableBy: ['patron'],
47	  handler: async (params) => {
48	    const cartograph = guild().apparatus<CartographApi>('cartograph');
49	    const clerk = guild().apparatus<ClerkApi>('clerk');
50	    const resolvedId = await clerk.resolveId(params.id);
51	    return cartograph.transitionVision(resolvedId, {
52	      phase: params.phase,
53	      stage: params.stage,
54	      ...(params.resolution !== undefined ? { resolution: params.resolution } : {}),
55	    });
56	  },
57	});

1	/**
2	 * Clerk public types.
3	 *
4	 * All types exported from @shardworks/clerk-apparatus.
5	 */
6	
7	import type {
8	  WritTypeConfig,
9	  WritTypeStateAttr,
10	  WritTypeStateClassification,
11	} from './writ-type-config.ts';
12	
13	// ── Writ phase ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
14	
15	/**
16	 * The mandate writ type's lifecycle states.
17	 *
18	 * This union is mandate-specific — it enumerates the six states the built-in
19	 * mandate `WritTypeConfig` declares and is preserved for callers that
20	 * knowingly work exclusively with mandate writs. The structural type of
21	 * `WritDoc.phase` is `string` so any plugin-registered writ type's state
22	 * name can round-trip through the book; callers that need a mandate-phase
23	 * typed value should downcast explicitly.
24	 *
25	 * Mandate transitions:
26	 *   new    → open       (publish)   — draft enters the queue
27	 *   new    → cancelled  (cancel)
28	 *   open   → completed  (complete)
29	 *   open   → failed     (fail)
30	 *   open   → cancelled  (cancel)
31	 *   open   → stuck      (engine failure cascade)
32	 *   stuck  → open       (recovery/retry resumes execution)
33	 *   stuck  → failed     (obligation abandoned)
34	 *   stuck  → cancelled  (obligation withdrawn)
35	 *
36	 * completed, failed, cancelled are terminal — no further transitions.
37	 * stuck is non-terminal — it represents a "needs attention" state.
38	 */
39	export type WritPhase = 'new' | 'open' | 'stuck' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled';
40	
41	// ── Documents ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42	
43	// ── status['clerk'] sub-slot ─────────────────────────────────────────
44	
45	/**
46	 * The Clerk-owned sub-slot of `WritDoc.status`.
47	 *
48	 * Written by the children-behavior cascade engine at the moment a parent's
49	 * terminal transition fires: the engine records the *immediate* triggering
50	 * child's id so downstream observers (notably the Reckoner) can chase the
51	 * cascade chain back to the leaf cause without parsing the parent's
52	 * resolution string.
53	 *
54	 * Ownership: Clerk writes; downstream consumers read. Plugin convention is
55	 * the same as for any `status[<pluginId>]` sub-slot — see
56	 * `setWritStatus()` and the spec/status convention notes on `ClerkApi`.
57	 *
58	 * The slot is forward-only: there is no migration or backfill for writs
59	 * that already terminal'd before this slot existed. Pre-existing terminal
60	 * writs do not re-emit pulses, so the absence of a slot on a historical
61	 * writ is harmless.
62	 */
63	export interface ClerkWritStatus {
64	  /**
65	   * Id of the immediate child whose terminal transition fired the
66	   * children-behavior cascade onto this writ. Absent on writs that
67	   * reached terminal through a direct transition (no cascade).
68	   *
69	   * For multi-level cascades (root → mid → leaf), each level's parent
70	   * carries its own immediate triggering child id; consumers walk the
71	   * chain by reading each successive writ's `status['clerk']
72	   * .triggeringChildId`.
73	   */
74	  triggeringChildId?: string;
75	}
76	
77	/**
78	 * Plugin id stamped on the Clerk's own observation sub-slot
79	 * (`status['clerk']`). Held as a constant so the engine and the
80	 * `setWritStatus` consumer agree on the key.
81	 */
82	export const CLERK_PLUGIN_ID = 'clerk';
83	
84	/**
85	 * A writ document as stored in The Stacks.
86	 */
87	export interface WritDoc {
88	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry constraint. */
89	  [key: string]: unknown;
90	  /** Unique writ id (`w-{base36_timestamp}-{hex_random}`). Sortable by creation time. */
91	  id: string;
92	  /** Writ type — must be a type declared in guild config, or a built-in type. */
93	  type: string;
94	  /**
95	   * Current lifecycle state name (Clerk-owned; spec side of the spec/status
96	   * split). Structurally typed as `string` so any plugin-registered writ
97	   * type's state name can round-trip through the book. For mandate-typed
98	   * writs the value is constrained to a `WritPhase` literal at runtime, but
99	   * callers that need a narrower static type should downcast explicitly.
100	   */
101	  phase: string;
102	  /**
103	   * Plugin-owned observation slot (status side of the spec/status split).
104	   *
105	   * The observation slot is a plugin-keyed map (`Record<PluginId,
106	   * unknown>`): each top-level key is a plugin id, and the value is an
107	   * arbitrary shape that plugin publishes for post-hoc observation.
108	   * Ownership is convention-only — plugin `X` writes only to `status[X]`.
109	   *
110	   * There is exactly one sanctioned slot-write path:
111	   * `ClerkApi.setWritStatus(writId, pluginId, value)`, which performs a
112	   * transactional read-modify-write on the sub-slot keyed by `pluginId`
113	   * so sibling sub-slots are preserved under concurrent writers.
114	   * `transition()` silently strips `status` from its body, and the
115	   * generic `put()` / `patch()` paths on the writs book are not
116	   * supported slot-write mechanisms — every route other than
117	   * `setWritStatus()` would wholesale-replace the slot and clobber
118	   * sibling sub-slots. Readers access `writ.status?.[pluginId]` directly.
119	   *
120	   * The slot survives terminal phase transitions.
121	   */
122	  status?: Record<string, unknown>;
123	  /**
124	   * Plugin-owned metadata slot — sibling to `status` but reserved for
125	   * metadata-shape data (provenance, cross-references, classification
126	   * tags, configuration extensions) attached at writ creation or
127	   * registration time, rather than the post-hoc observation that
128	   * `status` records.
129	   *
130	   * The semantic distinction matters: `status` is for what a plugin
131	   * has *observed* about a writ after the fact (a stuck cause, a
132	   * triggering child, a gate result); `ext` is for what a plugin needs
133	   * the writ to *carry* as an attribute of its identity (a petition id,
134	   * a classifier tag, a foreign-system reference). Picking the wrong
135	   * slot layers metadata under an observation contract or vice versa,
136	   * so plugin authors should choose consciously.
137	   *
138	   * Structurally identical to `status`: a plugin-keyed map
139	   * (`Record<PluginId, unknown>`) where each top-level key is a plugin
140	   * id and the value is an arbitrary opaque shape. Ownership is
141	   * convention-only — plugin `X` writes only to `ext[X]`.
142	   *
143	   * There is exactly one sanctioned slot-write path:
144	   * `ClerkApi.setWritExt(writId, pluginId, value)`, which performs a
145	   * transactional read-modify-write on the sub-slot keyed by `pluginId`
146	   * so sibling sub-slots are preserved under concurrent writers.
147	   * `transition()` silently strips `ext` from its body, and the
148	   * generic `put()` / `patch()` paths on the writs book are not
149	   * supported slot-write mechanisms — every route other than
150	   * `setWritExt()` would wholesale-replace the slot and clobber
151	   * sibling sub-slots. Readers access `writ.ext?.[pluginId]` directly.
152	   *
153	   * Optional and absent by default — a freshly-posted writ reads
154	   * `ext === undefined` until a plugin writes its first sub-slot. The
155	   * slot survives terminal phase transitions.
156	   */
157	  ext?: Record<string, unknown>;
158	  /** Short human-readable title. */
159	  title: string;
160	  /** Detail text. */
161	  body: string;
162	  /** Target codex name. */
163	  codex?: string;
164	  /** Parent writ id. Absent on root writs. Immutable after creation. */
165	  parentId?: string;
166	  /** ISO timestamp when the writ was created. */
167	  createdAt: string;
168	  /** ISO timestamp of the last mutation. */
169	  updatedAt: string;
170	  /** ISO timestamp when the writ reached a terminal state. */
171	  resolvedAt?: string;
172	  /** Summary of how the writ resolved (set on any terminal transition). */
173	  resolution?: string;
174	}
175	
176	// ── Requests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177	
178	/**
179	 * Request to post a new commission (create a writ).
180	 */
181	/**
182	 * Request to edit a writ. Title and body can be edited in any status.
183	 * Type and codex can only be changed while the writ is in 'new' status.
184	 * All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated.
185	 */
186	export interface EditWritRequest {
187	  /** Writ id. */
188	  id: string;
189	  /** New title. */
190	  title?: string;
191	  /** New body text. */
192	  body?: string;
193	  /** New writ type. Must be a valid declared type. */
194	  type?: string;
195	  /** New target codex name. Pass empty string to clear. */
196	  codex?: string;
197	}
198	
199	export interface PostCommissionRequest {
200	  /**
201	   * Writ type. Defaults to the guild's configured defaultType, or "mandate"
202	   * if no default is configured. Must be a registered writ type.
203	   */
204	  type?: string;
205	  /** Short human-readable title describing the work. */
206	  title: string;
207	  /** Detail text. */
208	  body: string;
209	  /** Optional target codex name. */
210	  codex?: string;
211	  /**
212	   * Create this writ as a child of the specified parent writ.
213	   * The parent must not be in a terminal state.
214	   */
215	  parentId?: string;
216	}
217	
218	// ── Filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
219	
220	/**
221	 * Filters for listing writs.
222	 */
223	export interface WritFilters {
224	  /**
225	   * Filter by phase. Accepts a single phase or an array of phases (OR).
226	   *
227	   * `phase` is mandate-scoped at the WHERE-clause level: when `phase` is
228	   * supplied without `type`, the implementation implicitly adds
229	   * `type = 'mandate'` so a non-mandate writ that happens to declare an
230	   * `open` state cannot match a `phase: 'open'` filter unscoped to its
231	   * type. To query a non-mandate type's same-named state, pass both
232	   * `type` and `phase` together (D7).
233	   */
234	  phase?: WritPhase | WritPhase[];
235	  /** Filter by writ type. Accepts a single type or an array of types (OR). */
236	  type?: string | string[];
237	  /**
238	   * Filter by state classification. Accepts a single classification or an
239	   * array (OR). Type-agnostic: applies across every registered writ type.
240	   * The closed three-value enum mirrors `WritTypeStateClassification`.
241	   */
242	  classification?:
243	    | WritTypeStateClassification
244	    | WritTypeStateClassification[];
245	  /** Filter to children of this parent writ. */
246	  parentId?: string;
247	  /** Maximum number of results (default: 20). */
248	  limit?: number;
249	  /** Number of results to skip. */
250	  offset?: number;
251	}
252	
253	// ── Presentation projections ─────────────────────────────────────────
254	
255	/**
256	 * Classification value as embedded on writ-bearing tool responses.
257	 *
258	 * Domain-side classifier helpers (`ClerkApi.isInitial`/`isActive`/
259	 * `isTerminal`) throw on an unregistered type or undeclared state — the
260	 * classifier preserves its fail-loud contract. The presentation projection
261	 * is tolerant and surfaces `'unknown'` for the same conditions so a single
262	 * legacy row cannot crash a list/tree/show response.
263	 */
264	export type WritPresentationClassification = 'initial' | 'active' | 'terminal' | 'unknown';
265	
266	/**
267	 * Per-row presentation projection. `writ-list` returns rows of this
268	 * shape; `writ-tree`'s `WritTree.writ` and `writ-show`'s top-level writ
269	 * extend `WritDoc` with the same two derived fields.
270	 */
271	export interface WritWithPresentation extends WritDoc {
272	  /**
273	   * Classification of the writ's current state in its type config.
274	   * `'unknown'` indicates the writ's type is unregistered or its phase
275	   * is not declared in the registered config (presentation tolerance).
276	   */
277	  classification: WritPresentationClassification;
278	  /**
279	   * Outbound transition names declared on the writ's current state in
280	   * its type config. Empty for terminal states and for unknown
281	   * classifications.
282	   */
283	  allowedTransitions: string[];
284	}
285	
286	/**
287	 * Compact parent/child reference shape used on `writ-show`'s `parent`
288	 * field and `children.items` entries. Carries the same `classification`
289	 * and `allowedTransitions` derivations as a full row so a renderer can
290	 * pick badge classes and action affordances uniformly.
291	 */
292	export interface WritReferenceWithPresentation {
293	  /** Writ id. */
294	  id: string;
295	  /** Writ title. */
296	  title: string;
297	  /** Writ type. */
298	  type: string;
299	  /** Current lifecycle state name. */
300	  phase: string;
301	  /** Classification of the writ's current state in its type config. */
302	  classification: WritPresentationClassification;
303	  /** Outbound transitions from the writ's current state. */
304	  allowedTransitions: string[];
305	}
306	
307	// ── Tree shapes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
308	
309	/**
310	 * A nested writ-and-children tree node, returned by `ClerkApi.tree()`.
311	 *
312	 * The shape is the writ document plus a recursively-typed `children` array
313	 * of further `WritTree` nodes. Mirrors ratchet's `ClickTree` so the same
314	 * mental model and rendering idioms apply across apparatus.
315	 *
316	 * Each node's `writ` carries the standard `WritDoc` fields plus the
317	 * presentation projection (`classification`, `allowedTransitions`) so
318	 * tree renderers can pick glyphs and action affordances per node without
319	 * reaching back into the type-config registry.
320	 */
321	export interface WritTree {
322	  /** The writ document at this node, with embedded presentation fields. */
323	  writ: WritWithPresentation;
324	  /** Direct children, each shaped as a `WritTree`. */
325	  children: WritTree[];
326	}
327	
328	/**
329	 * Parameters for `ClerkApi.tree()`.
330	 *
331	 * - `rootId` switches between forest mode (omit) and subtree mode
332	 *   (single root). In subtree mode the returned array always has at most
333	 *   one element.
334	 * - `phase` and `type` apply prune semantics: any node failing the filter
335	 *   is dropped along with its entire subtree.
336	 * - `depth` caps recursion. The node at depth = `depth` is included but its
337	 *   children are not. `depth: 0` returns roots only.
338	 * - `rootLimit` and `rootOffset` page across the *root* layer of forest
339	 *   mode (preserves the page's `Load more` UX). They are ignored when
340	 *   `rootId` is supplied.
341	 *
342	 * In forest mode roots are returned in `createdAt desc` order (newest
343	 * first) to match `list()` and the existing writs-page UX. Children
344	 * within each subtree stay in `createdAt asc` order (oldest first) so the
345	 * visual shape of the tree is stable across sort/filter changes.
346	 */
347	export interface WritTreeParams {
348	  /** Restrict to the subtree rooted at this writ id. */
349	  rootId?: string;
350	  /**
351	   * Filter by writ phase (single or OR-list); prunes non-matching
352	   * subtrees. Mandate-scoped at the same level as `WritFilters.phase` —
353	   * see that field's docstring (D7).
354	   */
355	  phase?: WritPhase | WritPhase[];
356	  /** Filter by writ type (single or OR-list); prunes non-matching subtrees. */
357	  type?: string | string[];
358	  /**
359	   * Filter by state classification (single or OR-list); prunes
360	   * non-matching subtrees. Type-agnostic — applies across every
361	   * registered writ type.
362	   */
363	  classification?:
364	    | WritTypeStateClassification
365	    | WritTypeStateClassification[];
366	  /** Maximum recursion depth (0 = roots only). Node at the cap is included. */
367	  depth?: number;
368	  /** Root-slice limit for forest mode (default: unbounded). */
369	  rootLimit?: number;
370	  /** Root-slice offset for forest mode (default: 0). */
371	  rootOffset?: number;
372	}
373	
374	// ── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────
375	
376	/**
377	 * Clerk apparatus configuration — lives under the `clerk` key in guild.json.
378	 *
379	 * Writ types are now contributed exclusively via
380	 * `ClerkApi.registerWritType` from a plugin's own `start()`. There is no
381	 * guild-config `writTypes` field; the operator's only handle on the writ-
382	 * type registry from guild config is the `defaultType` selection (which is
383	 * validated against the registry once startup seals).
384	 */
385	export interface ClerkConfig {
386	  /** Default writ type when commission-post is called without a type (default: "mandate"). Validated against the writ-type registry at startup; an unregistered name fails fast. */
387	  defaultType?: string;
388	}
389	
390	// Augment GuildConfig so `guild().guildConfig().clerk` is typed without
391	// requiring a manual type parameter at the call site.
392	declare module '@shardworks/nexus-core' {
393	  interface GuildConfig {
394	    clerk?: ClerkConfig;
395	  }
396	}
397	
398	// ── Link documents ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
399	
400	/**
401	 * A link document as stored in The Stacks (clerk/links book).
402	 *
403	 * A writ link has two complementary identifiers for its relationship:
404	 *
405	 *   - `label` is a casual, human-facing string. It is open-ended, normalized
406	 *     at write time via the link-label normalization pipeline (lowercase,
407	 *     trim, camelCase split, snake_case/kebab-case split, whitespace
408	 *     collapse). Variant spellings of the same label collapse to a single
409	 *     canonical form; distinct labels remain distinct. Normalization is
410	 *     purely syntactic — it is NOT synonymy. `requires` and `depends on`
411	 *     normalize to different strings; callers that want to treat them as the
412	 *     same relationship should attach the same `kind` to both.
413	 *
414	 *   - `kind` is the load-bearing identifier. It is a stable, plugin-owned id
415	 *     drawn from the kit-contributed link-kind registry, and identifies the
416	 *     specific relationship a downstream consumer wants to react to
417	 *     (rendering parent/child, gating publish on `refines`, etc.). `null`
418	 *     when the caller treated this link as a casual label.
419	 */
420	export interface WritLinkDoc {
421	  /** Index signature required to satisfy BookEntry constraint. */
422	  [key: string]: unknown;
423	  /** Deterministic composite key: `{sourceId}:{targetId}:{normalized label}`. */
424	  id: string;
425	  /** The writ that is the origin of this relationship. */
426	  sourceId: string;
427	  /** The writ that is the target of this relationship. */
428	  targetId: string;
429	  /**
430	   * Casual relationship label — an open string normalized syntactically at
431	   * write time (e.g. "fixes", "retries", "supersedes", "duplicates",
432	   * "depends on"). NOT a synonymy layer. For load-bearing identity, use
433	   * `kind`.
434	   */
435	  label: string;
436	  /**
437	   * Load-bearing identifier for this relationship. When non-null, references
438	   * a registered kind id from the `linkKinds` kit registry and is the field
439	   * downstream consumers key on. Always present on each row (`null` when
440	   * the caller did not attach a kind).
441	   */
442	  kind: string | null;
443	  /** ISO timestamp when the link was created. */
444	  createdAt: string;
445	}
446	
447	// ── Link kind registry ───────────────────────────────────────────────
448	
449	/**
450	 * A link-kind contribution declared by a kit's `linkKinds` array.
451	 *
452	 * Kit authors use this shape when populating `ClerkKit.linkKinds`. Kind ids
453	 * must be prefixed with the contributing plugin's id followed by `.` and a
454	 * kebab-case suffix — e.g. kit `astrolabe` contributing `astrolabe.refines`.
455	 * The registry-projection view (returned by `listKinds()`) is `LinkKindDoc`,
456	 * which embeds the resolved owner plugin id; kit authors do not repeat their
457	 * plugin id on each entry.
458	 */
459	export interface KindEntry {
460	  /**
461	   * Fully-qualified kind id. Must begin with `{pluginId}.` and be followed
462	   * by a non-empty kebab-case suffix (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens,
463	   * not starting or ending with a hyphen).
464	   *
465	   * Carve-out: contributions from the Clerk plugin itself (where the
466	   * contributing plugin id equals the Clerk's own id) may additionally use
467	   * a bare kebab id without a `{pluginId}.` prefix — e.g. `depends-on`.
468	   * The Clerk owns the writ link substrate and therefore owns the
469	   * unprefixed namespace within that substrate. The carve-out is exclusive
470	   * to Clerk; every other plugin must continue to use the prefixed form.
471	   */
472	  id: string;
473	  /** Human-readable description of the relationship this kind denotes. */
474	  description: string;
475	}
476	
477	/**
478	 * A link-kind record as projected by `listKinds()`.
479	 *
480	 * Unlike `KindEntry`, this shape embeds the resolved owner plugin id so
481	 * consumers of the registry do not need to parse it back out of `id`.
482	 */
483	export interface LinkKindDoc {
484	  /** Fully-qualified kind id. */
485	  id: string;
486	  /** Plugin id of the kit that contributed this kind. */
487	  ownerPlugin: string;
488	  /** Human-readable description of this kind. */
489	  description: string;
490	}
491	
492	/**
493	 * Result of querying links for a writ — both directions in one response.
494	 */
495	export interface WritLinks {
496	  /** Links where this writ is the source (this writ → other writ). */
497	  outbound: WritLinkDoc[];
498	  /** Links where this writ is the target (other writ → this writ). */
499	  inbound: WritLinkDoc[];
500	}
501	
502	// ── Writ type metadata ──────────────────────────────────────────────
503	
504	/**
505	 * Per-state metadata projected from a `WritTypeStateDefinition` for the
506	 * `/api/writ/types` and `nsg writ types` surfaces. Carries everything a
507	 * presentation layer needs to derive vocabulary, badges, and action
508	 * affordances without consulting the type-config registry per row.
509	 */
510	export interface WritTypeStateInfo {
511	  /** State name. */
512	  name: string;
513	  /** State's role in the lifecycle. */
514	  classification: WritTypeStateClassification;
515	  /** Per-state attribute tags (e.g. `success`, `failure`, `cancelled`). */
516	  attrs: WritTypeStateAttr[];
517	  /** Outbound transitions declared on this state. */
518	  allowedTransitions: string[];
519	}
520	
521	/**
522	 * Metadata for a registered writ type, returned by listWritTypes().
523	 */
524	export interface WritTypeInfo {
525	  /** The writ type name. */
526	  name: string;
527	  /**
528	   * Reserved for a future config-carried description field. Always `null`
529	   * today — `WritTypeConfig` does not currently model a description, and
530	   * the legacy guild-config / kit-channel description fields are gone.
531	   */
532	  description: string | null;
533	  /**
534	   * Origin of this type. `"builtin"` is reserved for `mandate`, which the
535	   * Clerk plugin registers from its own `start()`; every other registered
536	   * type carries `"plugin"`. The Clerk does not track the calling plugin's
537	   * id — there is no implicit hand-off of caller identity into
538	   * `registerWritType`.
539	   */
540	  source: 'builtin' | 'plugin';
541	  /** Whether this is the guild's default writ type. */
542	  isDefault: boolean;
543	  /**
544	   * The full state catalogue for this type — one entry per state declared
545	   * in the type's registered `WritTypeConfig`, in the order it was
546	   * declared. Each entry projects the state's name, classification, attrs,
547	   * and outbound transitions so a presentation layer can derive vocabulary
548	   * (badges, indicators, action labels) without a separate registry
549	   * lookup.
550	   */
551	  states: WritTypeStateInfo[];
552	}
553	
554	// ── API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
555	
556	/**
557	 * The Clerk's runtime API — retrieved via guild().apparatus<ClerkApi>('clerk').
558	 */
559	export interface ClerkApi {
560	  /**
561	   * Post a new commission, creating a writ in its registered type's
562	   * declared `initial` state. The writ type must be registered with
563	   * `registerWritType`; an unknown type is rejected. The caller decides
564	   * whether to advance the writ further (e.g. `commission-post` auto-
565	   * publishes mandate writs to `open` by default — see that tool); the
566	   * `post()` API itself is type-agnostic and never auto-advances.
567	   */
568	  post(request: PostCommissionRequest): Promise<WritDoc>;
569	
570	  /**
571	   * Show a writ by id. Throws if not found.
572	   */
573	  show(id: string): Promise<WritDoc>;
574	
575	  /**
576	   * Resolve a writ id prefix to the full id. Mirrors ratchet's resolveId
577	   * for clicks — callers may pass the short display form (e.g. `w-mo2xi6pt`)
578	   * and receive the full id back. Throws when no writ matches or when the
579	   * prefix is ambiguous.
580	   */
581	  resolveId(prefix: string): Promise<string>;
582	
583	  /**
584	   * List writs with optional filters, ordered by createdAt descending.
585	   */
586	  list(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<WritDoc[]>;
587	
588	  /**
589	   * Count writs matching optional filters.
590	   */
591	  count(filters?: WritFilters): Promise<number>;
592	
593	  /**
594	   * Count writs currently in any `active`-classified state across every
595	   * registered writ type.
596	   *
597	   * Walks the writ-type registry on each call (no caching) and composes a
598	   * per-type OR-form query of the shape `[type, phase IN [active-states-of-type]]`.
599	   * Returns `0` when the registry is empty, when no registered type
600	   * declares any `active` state, or when no rows match.
601	   *
602	   * This is the classification-driven primitive behind the Reckoner's
603	   * drain detector and any future apparatus that needs a multi-type
604	   * "is anything in flight?" count. Existing per-phase counts (e.g.
605	   * `count({ phase: 'open' })`) remain available for type-specific reads.
606	   */
607	  countActive(): Promise<number>;
608	
609	  /**
610	   * Walk the writ hierarchy and return a forest (or a single subtree when
611	   * `rootId` is provided). Mirrors ratchet's `tree()` precedent.
612	   *
613	   * Filters apply with prune semantics: a node failing `phase` or `type`
614	   * is excluded together with its entire subtree. The `depth` cap halts
615	   * recursion below — the node at the cap is still included, but no
616	   * descendants of it are walked. `rootLimit` / `rootOffset` page across
617	   * the root layer of forest mode and are ignored when `rootId` is given.
618	   */
619	  tree(params?: WritTreeParams): Promise<WritTree[]>;
620	
621	  /**
622	   * Count all descendants of a writ grouped by phase.
623	   *
624	   * Walks the parent/child tree recursively beneath `writId`, tallying each
625	   * descendant's phase. The root writ (the writ identified by `writId`) is
626	   * excluded from the count — only its descendants contribute. The result is
627	   * a plain object keyed by `WritPhase` with numeric values; phases with no
628	   * matching descendants are simply absent.
629	   *
630	   * This is the traversal primitive behind `writ-show`'s `children.summary`
631	   * field, and is also reusable by any future subtree-oriented tooling (e.g.
632	   * a `writ-tree` apparatus). Throws if the writ does not exist.
633	   */
634	  countDescendantsByPhase(writId: string): Promise<Record<WritPhase, number>>;
635	
636	  /**
637	   * Transition a writ to a new phase, optionally setting additional fields.
638	   * Validates that the transition is legal.
639	   *
640	   * Managed fields supplied in `fields` are silently dropped before the
641	   * patch is applied: `id`, `phase`, `status`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`,
642	   * `resolvedAt`, and `parentId`. `phase` is managed by the state machine;
643	   * the plugin-owned observation slot `status` is writable only via
644	   * `setWritStatus()` (the one sanctioned slot-write path) so that
645	   * sibling sub-slots are preserved under concurrent writers. The generic
646	   * `put()` / `patch()` paths are not supported slot-write mechanisms.
647	   */
648	  transition(id: string, to: WritPhase, fields?: Partial<WritDoc>): Promise<WritDoc>;
649	
650	  /**
651	   * Write a plugin-owned sub-slot of the writ's observation `status` map.
652	   *
653	   * The slot is the "status" side of the spec/status split: plugins publish
654	   * post-hoc observations here (stuck causes, gate state, provenance, …)
655	   * without polluting the Clerk-owned `phase` state machine.
656	   *
657	   * The call is a transactional read-modify-write — the sub-slot keyed by
658	   * `pluginId` is replaced with `value`, but sibling sub-slots owned by
659	   * other plugins are preserved. Writes emit CDC update events like any
660	   * other field change, and survive terminal phase transitions.
661	   *
662	   * Ownership is convention-only: plugin `X` writes only `status[X]`.
663	   * There is no runtime guard.
664	   *
665	   * Returns the updated writ document.
666	   */
667	  setWritStatus(writId: string, pluginId: string, value: unknown): Promise<WritDoc>;
668	
669	  /**
670	   * Write a plugin-owned sub-slot of the writ's metadata `ext` map.
671	   *
672	   * The slot is the metadata sibling to `status`: plugins attach
673	   * metadata-shape data here (petition ids, cross-references,
674	   * classifier tags, configuration extensions) — data that belongs to
675	   * the writ as an attribute of its identity, rather than the post-hoc
676	   * observation `setWritStatus` records.
677	   *
678	   * The call is a transactional read-modify-write — the sub-slot keyed by
679	   * `pluginId` is replaced with `value`, but sibling sub-slots owned by
680	   * other plugins are preserved. Writes emit CDC update events like any
681	   * other field change, and survive terminal phase transitions.
682	   *
683	   * Validation: throws when `writId` is empty, when `pluginId` is empty,
684	   * or when no writ matches `writId`. The `value` is opaque — the Clerk
685	   * does not validate sub-slot contents (mirrors `setWritStatus`).
686	   *
687	   * Each call bumps `updatedAt` to the current ISO timestamp so CDC
688	   * observers keying on the timestamp see the change.
689	   *
690	   * Ownership is convention-only: plugin `X` writes only `ext[X]`.
691	   * There is no runtime guard.
692	   *
693	   * Returns the updated writ document.
694	   */
695	  setWritExt(writId: string, pluginId: string, value: unknown): Promise<WritDoc>;
696	
697	  /**
698	   * Create a typed directional link from one writ to another.
699	   * Both writs must exist. Self-links are rejected. The `label` argument is
700	   * normalized before the composite id is constructed, so variant spellings
701	   * of the same label collapse to a single link. When a link with the same
702	   * canonical composite id already exists, the existing row is returned;
703	   * if `kind` is supplied, it is written onto the existing row (upsert). The
704	   * optional `kind` must reference an id registered in the kit-contributed
705	   * link-kind registry; unknown ids are rejected.
706	   */
707	  link(
708	    sourceId: string,
709	    targetId: string,
710	    label: string,
711	    kind?: string,
712	  ): Promise<WritLinkDoc>;
713	
714	  /**
715	   * Query all links for a writ — both outbound (this writ is the source)
716	   * and inbound (this writ is the target).
717	   */
718	  links(writId: string): Promise<WritLinks>;
719	
720	  /**
721	   * Remove a link. Idempotent — no error if the link does not exist.
722	   */
723	  unlink(sourceId: string, targetId: string, label: string): Promise<void>;
724	
725	  /**
726	   * Edit a writ. Title and body can be edited in any status.
727	   * Type and codex can only be changed while the writ is in 'new' status.
728	   * Only the provided fields are updated. Validates type against
729	   * declared writ types if provided.
730	   */
731	  edit(request: EditWritRequest): Promise<WritDoc>;
732	
733	  /**
734	   * List the writ types registered with the Clerk via `registerWritType`,
735	   * including the Clerk's own built-in `mandate`. Each entry reports the
736	   * name, the (currently-always-null) description, source (`builtin` or
737	   * `plugin`), and whether it is the default type.
738	   */
739	  listWritTypes(): WritTypeInfo[];
740	
741	  /**
742	   * List all registered link kinds contributed by kits.
743	   * Returns the registry-projection view of each kind, including its owner
744	   * plugin id. Returns an empty array when no kinds have been registered.
745	   */
746	  listKinds(): Promise<LinkKindDoc[]>;
747	
748	  /**
749	   * Register a writ type's state machine with the Clerk.
750	   *
751	   * The supplied config is validated via `validateWritTypeConfig` before the
752	   * registry is updated; validator errors propagate verbatim. Registration-
753	   * specific failures (duplicate name, late call after the startup window has
754	   * sealed) are wrapped with a `[clerk] registerWritType:` prefix.
755	   *
756	   * Callable only while the startup window is open — Clerk seals the
757	   * registry on the framework's global `phase:started` signal, after which
758	   * further calls throw. This is the single-surface entry point for plugins
759	   * contributing writ types; there is no parallel kit-channel or guild-config
760	   * registration path.
761	   */
762	  registerWritType(config: WritTypeConfig): void;
763	
764	  /**
765	   * Return the registered `WritTypeConfig` for a writ type.
766	   *
767	   * Returns `undefined` when the name is not registered. Callers that need
768	   * to work with a writ type abstractly (inspect states, render UI labels,
769	   * compose higher-level predicates) should use this accessor instead of
770	   * reaching into the predicate surface with synthetic `WritDoc`s.
771	   */
772	  getWritTypeConfig(name: string): WritTypeConfig | undefined;
773	
774	  /**
775	   * Return `true` when the writ's current state is classified `initial`
776	   * in its type's registered config.
777	   *
778	   * Throws when the writ's stored state is not declared in its type config
779	   * (fail-loud on unknown-state), or when the writ's type is not registered.
780	   */
781	  isInitial(writ: WritDoc): boolean;
782	
783	  /**
784	   * Return `true` when the writ's current state is classified `active`
785	   * in its type's registered config.
786	   *
787	   * Throws when the writ's stored state is not declared in its type config
788	   * (fail-loud on unknown-state), or when the writ's type is not registered.
789	   */
790	  isActive(writ: WritDoc): boolean;
791	
792	  /**
793	   * Return `true` when the writ's current state is classified `terminal`
794	   * in its type's registered config.
795	   *
796	   * Throws when the writ's stored state is not declared in its type config
797	   * (fail-loud on unknown-state), or when the writ's type is not registered.
798	   */
799	  isTerminal(writ: WritDoc): boolean;
800	}

1	# The Surveying Cascade — Patron-Vision Authoring through Mandate Dispatch
2	
3	Status: **Draft**
4	
5	> **⚠️ Cross-apparatus design.** This document defines the
6	> end-to-end cascade by which patron-authored product visions become
7	> commissionable mandate work, spanning three apparatuses: the
8	> Cartograph (data layer, shipped), the Surveyor (substrate +
9	> extension + default, not yet built), and the Reckoner (petition
10	> scheduler, shipped). It is a settled architectural reference; the
11	> open questions section names the design problems that remain.
12	
13	---
14	
15	## Scope
16	
17	The cascade covers:
18	
19	- **Vision authoring on disk** — patrons author visions as `vision.md`
20	  files in a guild directory tree; `nsg vision apply` snapshots them
21	  into the cartograph.
22	- **Recursive surveying** — typed survey writs at every cartograph
23	  layer (vision/charge/piece) gate the work of decomposing one node
24	  into its children, using the Reckoner for scheduling.
25	- **Mandate emission** — surveying piece-layer (or charge-layer)
26	  rigs ultimately produce mandates, which dispatch through the
27	  existing implementer pipeline unchanged.
28	
29	Out of scope: the patron walkthrough surface (the patron-contract
30	validation step at the charge layer) is acknowledged as a
31	substantial design problem but designed elsewhere.
32	
33	---
34	
35	## 1. Vocabulary
36	
37	| Concept | Definition |
38	|---|---|
39	| **Vision** | Long-lived patron intent. Authored on disk; snapshotted into a writ + VisionDoc by the cartograph. |
40	| **Charge** | First decomposition under a vision. Patron-contract boundary; the unit of patron walkthrough. Does NOT self-nest. |
41	| **Piece** | Recursive internal organization under a charge. Self-nests. No patron contract. |
42	| **Mandate** | Existing leaf writ; dispatched by Spider via the existing implementer pipeline. |
43	| **Survey writ** (`survey-vision`/`survey-charge`/`survey-piece`) | Typed writ that captures one act of surveying one cartograph node. `writ.body` holds the rig's notes (set on rig completion). One survey writ per surveying event. |
44	| **SurveyDoc** | Companion doc for a survey writ, keyed by writ id. Holds envelope metadata only — the rig's notes live in `writ.body`. |
45	| **Surveyor** | The role / agent / apparatus that performs surveying. The substrate provides the cascade machinery; concrete surveyor implementations register their rig templates as kit contributions. |
46	| **`ext['surveyor']`** | Plugin-keyed `writ.ext` slot owned by the surveyor-apparatus. Carries priority hints (severity, deadline, decay, complexity) on cartograph nodes — set by the patron via apply CLI for visions, set by the upstream surveyor rig for charges and pieces. The substrate translates these into Reckoner dimensions when emitting the survey petition. |
47	
48	> **Naming note.** "The Surveyor" was previously reserved in the
49	> framework architecture for a codex-awareness apparatus. That use is
50	> superseded by the cartograph-decomposition meaning defined here. The
51	> alias registry in the sanctum carries the substitution.
52	
53	---
54	
55	## 2. End-to-end flow
56	
57	```
58	PATRON
59	  │ writes vision.md + sidecar in <GUILD>/vision/<slug>/
60	  │
61	  │ runs `nsg vision apply <slug> [--severity ...] [--deadline ...]`
62	  ▼
63	CARTOGRAPH (data layer)
64	  │ creates or updates the vision writ + VisionDoc (one transaction)
65	  │ writes ext['surveyor'] priority hints from CLI flags + sidecar
66	  │ emits Stacks CDC: book.cartograph.visions.{created,updated}
67	  ▼
68	SURVEYOR-APPARATUS (substrate)
69	  │ CDC observer fires
70	  │ reads ext['surveyor'] hints from the cartograph node
71	  │ creates a survey-vision writ in `new` phase, parentId = the vision
72	  │ stamps ext['reckoner'] (priority dimensions derived from hints + defaults)
73	  ▼
74	RECKONER
75	  │ holds petition, evaluates per tick
76	  │ dependency-aware gating; scheduler decides accept/decline/defer
77	  │ on accept: clerk.transition → survey-vision writ moves to `open`
78	  ▼
79	SPIDER (extended to dispatch survey-* writ types)
80	  │ resolves rig template via the surveyor registry
81	  │ dispatches to the registered surveyor's rig
82	  ▼
83	SURVEYOR RIG (provided by the registered surveyor implementation)
84	  │ reads parent (vision) via writ.parentId
85	  │ reads existing children if any (re-survey case)
86	  │ reasons about decomposition
87	  │ creates child charges via cartograph API + sets ext['surveyor'] hints
88	  │ writes its notes into survey-vision writ.body
89	  │ completes the survey-vision writ
90	  ▼
91	[CASCADE]
92	  │ each new charge fires CDC → substrate creates survey-charge writ
93	  │ → recursion descends one layer
94	  ▼
95	LEAF (mandate)
96	  │ pieces eventually elaborate to mandates
97	  │ mandates dispatch through the existing implementer pipeline
98	```
99	
100	---
101	
102	## 3. Architecture
103	
104	### 3.1 Vision authoring on disk
105	
106	The patron's authoring surface is a directory tree:
107	
108	```
109	<GUILD>/vision/
110	  <slug>/
111	    vision.md             ← prose, patron-edited
112	    vision-metadata.yml   ← sidecar; carries `visionId` after first apply
113	```
114	
115	- The slug is a directory affordance, not a durable identity. After
116	  first apply, the sidecar's `visionId` field binds the file tree to
117	  its cartograph writ — written by the system, never edited by hand.
118	- `nsg vision apply <slug>` is a single code path for both first
119	  import and Nth re-import: locate sidecar, resolve bound writ via
120	  `visionId` (or create new), copy prose into `writ.body`, sync
121	  stage/codex via `transitionVision`, write priority hints into
122	  `ext['surveyor']`, and on first run write the new id back into the
123	  sidecar.
124	- Data flow is **one-way: file → writ**. The writ is a snapshot of
125	  patron intent; the file is the editable source.
126	- Stale-binding (file deleted, writ remains): warn at scan, error on
127	  next apply attempt; the patron transitions the writ to
128	  sunset/cancelled explicitly.
129	
130	### 3.2 Symmetric typed-writ-driven cascade
131	
132	Surveying happens through dedicated survey writs at every cartograph
133	layer. The cascade has a uniform shape:
134	
135	1. A cartograph node lands (vision via apply; charge or piece via an
136	   upstream survey rig).
137	2. CDC fires on the cartograph book — `created` or `updated`.
138	3. The surveyor-apparatus's observer reads `ext['surveyor']` from the
139	   node and creates the appropriate survey writ in `new` phase with
140	   `parentId` set to the cartograph node. Priority hints translate
141	   into Reckoner dimensions.
142	4. The observer stamps `ext['reckoner']` to enter the petition queue.
143	5. Reckoner gates per its existing rules.
144	6. On accept, Spider dispatches the registered surveyor's rig.
145	7. Rig reads the parent (and existing children, on re-survey),
146	   reasons about decomposition, creates children via the surveyor
147	   tool surface (which atomically creates the cartograph node and
148	   stamps its `ext['surveyor']`), writes its notes into the survey
149	   writ's `body`, completes.
150	8. Each child cartograph node fires fresh CDC → loop to step 2 one
151	   layer down.
152	
153	Why symmetric (separate survey writs at every layer) over asymmetric
154	(rigs bound directly to charge/piece writs): notes have a clean home
155	(`writ.body` of a dedicated writ per surveying event); charge/piece
156	writ lifecycle stays clean (their phase is about contract lifecycle,
157	not about whether decomposition has happened); future re-survey of an
158	existing charge is structurally trivial; substrate code is uniform
159	across layers. The cost — one extra writ per cartograph node and three
160	new writ types — is bounded; survey writs reach terminal phase quickly.
161	
162	### 3.3 Survey writs carry the rig's notes in `writ.body`
163	
164	| Pattern | `writ.body` is... | Set when |
165	|---|---|---|
166	| Mandate | the brief (instructions to the rig) | creation |
167	| Vision/charge/piece | the canonical content (prose, the thing itself) | creation/apply |
168	| Survey writ | the rig's notes (canonical content of the act of surveying) | rig completion |
169	
170	The survey writ IS the act of surveying. Its content IS the
171	surveyor's notes — the reasoning trace, the considered alternatives,
172	the rationale for the structural decisions made. Body is empty
173	pre-completion; the rig fills it on completion.
174	
175	### 3.4 Companion `SurveyDoc` holds envelope metadata only
176	
177	```typescript
178	interface SurveyDoc {
179	  id: string;             // primary key — the survey writ id
180	  targetNodeId: string;   // the cartograph node being surveyed
181	  rigName: string;        // 'survey-vision' | 'survey-charge' | 'survey-piece'
182	  rigVersion: string;     // for replay / diffing across rig versions
183	  surveyorId: string;     // which surveyor implementation ran
184	  completedAt: string;    // ISO timestamp
185	  // notes are NOT here — they live in writ.body
186	}
187	```
188	
189	Owned by the surveyor-apparatus substrate; shared across all surveyor
190	implementations.
191	
192	### 3.5 Immutability and supersedes
193	
194	Charges and pieces are rig-produced and never edited in place. When a
195	vision change cascades down such that an existing charge needs to
196	change, the rig creates a new charge with a `supersedes:
197	<oldChargeId>` link. The old charge stays in the books with its
198	content intact, marked superseded.
199	
200	| Substrate codifies | Surveyor implementation decides |
201	|---|---|
202	| `supersedes` link kind exists and is queryable | Whether to supersede vs keep |
203	| `cartograph.createCharge({ ..., supersedes })` API supports the link atomically | What threshold of vision-change triggers supersede |
204	| Re-survey rigs receive existing children as input context | How aggressive to be (rewrite vs conserve) |
205	| Superseded charges remain queryable; their dispatched work continues | Whether superseding cascades to children — substrate default: no auto-cascade |
206	| The survey writ records the rig's decision in its notes | Stop conditions, reasoning approach |
207	
208	Pieces follow the same mechanism. Mandates stay where they are.
209	
210	### 3.6 Substrate watches cartograph CDC — single-event-per-apply guarantee
211	
212	The substrate's observer subscribes to:
213	
214	- `book.cartograph.visions.created` / `.updated`
215	- `book.cartograph.charges.created` / `.updated`
216	- `book.cartograph.pieces.created`  / `.updated`
217	
218	On any event, it reads `ext['surveyor']` hints from the affected
219	node, creates the appropriate survey writ with `parentId` pointing
220	to the node, derives `ext['reckoner']` priority dimensions from the
221	hints + substrate defaults, and stamps to enter the petition queue.
222	
223	**Single-event-per-apply guarantee.** Apply CLI and surveyor rigs
224	MUST produce a single CDC event per logical operation, not separate
225	create+transition events. Concretely:
226	
227	- `cartograph.createVision` (and `createCharge`, `createPiece`) must
228	  accept the initial stage as a parameter and write writ + companion
229	  doc + final stage in one Stacks transaction, OR the apply CLI must
230	  wrap create + transition in one transaction at its layer.
231	- The substrate observer dedupes by writ id within a short window
232	  (defensive — should never matter if upstream is transactional, but
233	  catches integration bugs cheaply).
234	
235	Without this, a single `nsg vision apply` produces two CDC fires
236	(create + transition-to-active) and the substrate creates two
237	identical survey writs — wasted Reckoner cycles and duplicate rig
238	dispatches. This is a correctness requirement, not an optimization.
239	
240	### 3.7 Substrate + extension + default plugin shape
241	
242	Three plugins, mirroring the Reckoner pattern:
243	
244	1. **`@shardworks/cartograph-apparatus`** *(shipped)* — pure data layer.
245	   Vision/charge/piece writ types + companion docs + ladder-invariant
246	   API. Does not own surveying.
247	
248	2. **`@shardworks/surveyor-apparatus`** *(substrate)*
249	   - Owns survey writ types and `books.surveys`
250	   - Owns the surveyor registry (kit-contribution surface)
251	   - Owns the CDC observer
252	   - Owns the `ext['surveyor']` slot
253	   - Owns the rig-name convention (`survey-vision/charge/piece`)
254	   - Routes accepted survey petitions to the registered surveyor's
255	     rig templates
256	   - Stamps SurveyDoc on completion (rig fills `writ.body`; substrate
257	     wraps the writ)
258	   - Provides the surveyor anima tool surface (see §3.9)
259	   - Does NOT ship a concrete surveyor.
260	
261	3. **`@shardworks/scaffold-surveyor`** *(default)*
262	   - Registers a surveyor with the substrate via kit contribution
263	   - Provides minimal LLM-driven rig templates for each cartograph
264	     layer
265	   - Useful immediately; designed to be replaced as approaches are
266	     iterated
267	
268	### 3.8 Spider's dispatchable type set extends
269	
270	Spider's dispatchable type set grows to include survey writ types in
271	addition to mandates. Existing dispatch mechanics compose unchanged —
272	Spider already knows how to read writ type and resolve a rig
273	template; the extension is the registry the substrate provides for
274	discovering the registered surveyor's templates dynamically.
275	
276	### 3.9 Tool surface for surveyor anima — atomic create-with-hints
277	
278	Anima rigs need atomic operations that bundle the cartograph create
279	call, the `ext['surveyor']` stamping, and any `supersedes` link in
280	one tool call wrapped in a Stacks transaction. The surveyor-apparatus
281	contributes these tools.
282	
283	#### Tools (callableBy: `['surveyor']`)
284	
285	```
286	surveyor.create_charge / create_charges       # for survey-vision rigs
287	surveyor.create_piece  / create_pieces        # for survey-charge / -piece rigs
288	surveyor.create_mandate / create_mandates     # for survey-charge / -piece rigs
289	```
290	
291	Each rig template's tool catalogue includes only the layer-relevant
292	subset.
293	
294	**Single-create shape (charge example):**
295	
296	```typescript
297	interface CreateChargeArgs {
298	  parentId: string;              // vision id
299	  title: string;
300	  body: string;
301	  codex?: string;
302	  hints?: SurveyorExt;           // { severity, deadline, decay, complexity }
303	  supersedes?: string;           // optional — if replacing an existing charge
304	}
305	```
306	
307	Internally wraps a single `stacks.transaction`:
308	1. `cartograph.createCharge` (typed API)
309	2. `clerk.setWritExt('surveyor', hints)` if hints present
310	3. `clerk.linkWrits(newId, supersedes, 'supersedes')` if supersedes present
311	
312	**Batch-create shape:**
313	
314	```typescript
315	interface CreateChargesArgs {
316	  parentId: string;              // common parent — all in one batch share it
317	  charges: Array<{
318	    title: string;
319	    body: string;
320	    codex?: string;
321	    hints?: SurveyorExt;
322	    supersedes?: string;
323	  }>;
324	}
325	```
326	
327	Common parent enforced per batch: all children in one batch are
328	siblings under a single parent. Matches how surveys actually
329	decompose.
330	
331	**Mandate-create shape — direct ext.reckoner stamping:**
332	
333	```typescript
334	interface CreateMandateArgs {
335	  parentId: string;              // charge or piece
336	  title: string;
337	  body: string;                  // the brief content
338	  codex?: string;
339	  reckonerHints?: ReckonerExtRequest;  // priority dimensions for Reckoner gating
340	}
341	```
342	
343	Mandates aren't surveyed, so they skip `ext['surveyor']`. The tool
344	calls `reckoner.petition(writId, ext)` (stamp-only form) directly.
345	
346	#### Read tools
347	
348	The cartograph's existing `show`/`list` tools serve surveyor anima
349	needs; the only change is widening `callableBy` to include
350	`'surveyor'`.
351	
352	### 3.10 Priority signals — substrate defaults + patron flags + rig hints
353	
354	Priority dimensions for survey petitions are layered:
355	
356	```
357	substrate defaults  ◄──  rig hints (ext['surveyor'])  ◄──  patron CLI flags
358	       (vision-apply only; merges into ext['surveyor'])
359	```
360	
361	For visions, the patron sets priority via `nsg vision apply` CLI
362	flags (or sidecar fields). For charges and pieces, the surveyor rig
363	that creates the new node sets `ext['surveyor']` hints during
364	creation — the rig has the most context about this particular
365	charge/piece's importance. The substrate reads `ext['surveyor']` when
366	emitting each layer's survey petition.
367	
368	#### Patron CLI surface
369	
370	```
371	nsg vision apply <slug>
372	   [--severity moderate|serious|critical]    # default: moderate
373	   [--deadline <ISO-date>]                   # default: none
374	   [--decay]                                  # default: false
375	```
376	
377	#### Substrate dimension defaults (when hints absent)
378	
379	| Dimension | Default | Patron-overridable? | Rig-overridable? |
380	|---|---|---|---|
381	| `visionRelation` | `vision-advancer` for fresh; `vision-violator` for re-survey detecting contradiction | No | Yes (rig assesses on re-survey) |
382	| `severity` | `moderate` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].severity`) |
383	| `scope` | layer-based: vision=`major-area`, charge=`minor-area`, piece=`trivial` | No | No (substrate-derived from layer) |
384	| `time.decay` | `false` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].decay`) |
385	| `time.deadline` | `null` | Yes (CLI flag) | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].deadline`) |
386	| `domain` | `['planning']` | No | No |
387	| `complexity` | `bounded` | No | Yes (`ext['surveyor'].complexity`) |
388	
389	#### `ext['surveyor']` shape
390	
391	```typescript
392	interface SurveyorExt {
393	  severity?: 'moderate' | 'serious' | 'critical';
394	  deadline?: string;       // ISO date
395	  decay?: boolean;
396	  complexity?: 'bounded' | 'unbounded';
397	}
398	```
399	
400	Substrate-owned plugin-keyed slot on `writ.ext`. The vision-apply
